content.title: Power Series
content.content:
A **power series** centered at $c$ is a series 
of the form
\[
\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} a_n(x - c)^n.
\]

content.title: Radius of Convergence
content.content:
For any power series $\sum a_n(x - c)^n$, there exists $R \in [0, \infty]$ 
such that:

1. The series converges absolutely for $|x - c| < R$.

2. The series diverges for $|x - c| > R$.
This $R$ is called the **radius of convergence**.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Let $R = \sup\{|x - c| : \sum a_n(x-c)^n \text{ converges}\}$.

If $|x - c| < R$, choose $y$ with $|x - c| < |y - c| < R$ and $\sum a_n(y-c)^n$ 
converging. Then $a_n(y-c)^n \to 0$, so $|a_n(y-c)^n| \leq M$ for some $M$.

Thus $|a_n(x-c)^n| = |a_n(y-c)^n| \cdot |x-c|^n/|y-c|^n \leq M r^n$ where 
$r = |x-c|/|y-c| < 1$. By comparison with geometric series, $\sum a_n(x-c)^n$ 
converges absolutely.

content.title: Uniform Convergence of Power Series
content.content:
The power series $\sum a_n(x - c)^n$ converges uniformly on any closed 
interval $[c - r, c + r]$ with $0 < r < R$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Power Series Are Infinitely Differentiable
content.content:
If $f(x) = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} a_n(x - c)^n$ for $|x - c| < R$, then:

1. $f$ is infinitely differentiable on $(c - R, c + R)$.

2. $f'(x) = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} n a_n(x - c)^{n-1}$.

3. $\int f(x)\,dx = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{a_n}{n+1}(x - c)^{n+1} + C$.

4. Both derived series have the same radius of convergence $R$.

5. $a_n = \frac{f^{(n)}(c)}{n!}$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Exponential Series
content.problem:
The series $e^x = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{x^n}{n!}$ has $R = \infty$.

By the ratio test: $\left|\frac{a_{n+1}}{a_n}\right| = \frac{1}{n+1} \to 0$.

content.title: Geometric Series
content.problem:
The series $\frac{1}{1-x} = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} x^n$ has $R = 1$.

Differentiating: $\frac{1}{(1-x)^2} = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} n x^{n-1}$.
